# Decisions: .NET / C#
> Stack: dotnet | Status: DEFERRED | Applicable: NO
> Deferred-Reason: Zero .cs files, no .csproj, no NuGet packages. Codebase is pure JavaScript. Activate when .NET components are added.

---

## Decided Items

| ID | Priority | Scope | Decision | Notes | Date |
|----|-----------|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| DEC-150 | HIGH | Phase 2 (.NET Runtime Baseline) | Must all new services and libraries target .NET 10 (`net10.0`) by default, with multi-targeting allowed only by exception? | Yes. Default target is net10.0 for all new code; multi-targeting requires documented compatibility need and explicit approval. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-151 | HIGH | Phase 2 (SDK Pinning) | What SDK pinning policy applies for builds (global.json version, roll-forward rules, preview SDK usage)? | Use global.json pinned to approved .NET 10 SDK patch; roll-forward limited to patch updates; preview SDKs are prohibited in CI and production branches. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-152 | HIGH | Phase 2 (C# Compiler Strictness) | What mandatory compiler defaults are required (nullable, implicit usings, language version, warnings as errors)? | Mandatory: nullable enabled, implicit usings enabled, language version set to latest major shipped with pinned SDK, and warnings treated as errors in CI. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-153 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Analyzers & Code Quality) | Which analyzer/rule set is mandatory and blocking for merge (Microsoft analyzers, custom rules, suppression policy)? | Microsoft .NET analyzers are mandatory and blocking; suppressions require inline justification and reviewer approval; new warnings cannot be introduced. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-154 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Dependency Management) | Is Central Package Management with lock files required, and what is the dependency update cadence? | Yes. Use Central Package Management and NuGet lock files in CI; dependency update cadence is weekly for non-breaking updates and monthly review for minor/major upgrades. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-155 | HIGH | Phase 2 (NuGet Vulnerability Policy) | What vulnerability severity thresholds block PR merge and release for direct/transitive packages? | Block merge/release on any Critical or High vulnerability; Medium requires tracked remediation plan with due date; exceptions require security waiver. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-156 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Solution Architecture) | Which project architecture convention is mandatory for C# solutions (layering boundaries, shared kernel rules, anti-corruption boundaries)? | Enforce clean layered architecture (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, API) with strict dependency direction inward; shared kernel minimized; external integrations isolated behind anti-corruption adapters. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-157 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (API Compatibility) | What backward-compatibility and versioning policy is required for public APIs and contracts? | Public APIs follow semantic versioning; non-breaking changes only in minor/patch releases; breaking changes require major version and published migration notes before release. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-158 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Serialization Policy) | Which serialization defaults are required (System.Text.Json options, casing, enum handling, date/time format, strictness)? | Standardize on System.Text.Json with camelCase properties, string enums, ISO-8601 UTC timestamps, and strict unknown-field handling for internal contracts unless explicitly versioned. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-159 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Async & Resilience) | What async/cancellation/timeout standards are mandatory for I/O and external calls? | All I/O must be async; public async methods require CancellationToken unless technically infeasible; outbound calls must define explicit timeout and retry policy. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-160 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Error Handling) | What exception-handling contract is required for APIs and background jobs (ProblemDetails, correlation IDs, retryability classification)? | APIs must return standardized ProblemDetails; errors must include correlation IDs in logs; transient vs non-transient classification must drive retry behavior. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-161 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Observability) | What logging/metrics/tracing baseline is mandatory for .NET services (structured logs, OpenTelemetry, redaction rules)? | Require structured logging with correlation IDs, OpenTelemetry traces/metrics enabled by default, and mandatory redaction of secrets/PII in logs. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-162 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Testing Gates) | What mandatory test gates apply for C# code before merge/deploy (unit/integration coverage thresholds and smoke checks)? | Block merge unless unit tests pass with minimum 80% line coverage; changed integration tests must pass; deployment requires post-deploy smoke test pass. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-163 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Performance Standards) | What performance budgets and benchmark requirements are mandatory for critical paths? | Define SLO-backed latency/error budgets per critical endpoint; benchmark critical paths before release and block if p95 latency regresses by more than 10% without approved waiver. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-164 | HIGH | Phase 2 (Secure Coding) | Which secure coding controls are mandatory in C# (no custom crypto, input validation, safe deserialization, parameterized queries)? | No custom cryptography; validate all external input; use safe serializers and parameterized data access; security-critical changes require security review before merge. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-165 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Container Runtime) | What base image/runtime policy is required for .NET services (image family, patch cadence, CVE response SLA)? | Use official Microsoft .NET runtime images (non-preview) with monthly patch refresh; Critical CVEs remediated within 48 hours and High within 7 days. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-166 | LOW | Phase 2 (AOT/Trimming) | Is Native AOT or trimming required/allowed, and what compatibility test requirements apply before adoption? | AOT/trimming is optional and allowed by exception; adoption requires compatibility validation, startup/perf benchmark evidence, and rollback plan before production use. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-167 | MEDIUM | Phase 2 (Support & Deprecation) | What support lifecycle and deprecation policy is required for internal libraries/packages? | Internal packages require at least 12 months support from latest release; deprecations require notice in release notes and a migration window of minimum 2 minor releases. | 2026-03-07 |
| DEC-168 | LOW | Phase 2 (Developer Experience) | Which local developer workflow standards are mandatory (dotnet format, pre-commit hooks, local test command baseline)? | Require dotnet format, pre-commit lint/test hooks, and a documented one-command local validation workflow matching CI baseline checks. | 2026-03-07 |
